2605.02682.title: Hybrid Inspection and Task-Based Access Control in Zero-Trust Agentic AI
2605.02682.paper_summary: Authorizing Large Language Model (LLM)-driven agents to dynamically invoke tools and access protected resources introduces significant security risks, and the risks grow dramatically as agents engage in multi-turn conversations and scale toward distributed collaboration. A compromised or malicious agentic application can tamper with tool calls, falsify results, or request permissions beyond the scope of the subject's intended tasks, which could go unnoticed with current delegated authorization flows given their lack of visibility into the original subject's intent. In light of this, we make the following contributions towards Continuous Agent Semantic Authorization (CASA). First, we propose a hybrid runtime enforcement model that combines deterministic and semantic controls enabled by a zero-trust interception layer. Five deterministic controls enforce structural and data-integrity guarantees over the message flow, while a semantic inspection layer evaluates whether tool call choices align with the intended tasks commissioned to the agent. Second, differently from prior Task-Based Access Control (TBAC) techniques that operate on single-turn interactions, we decompose the semantic layer into two stages: i) a task-extraction step that distills the subject's objectives from multi-turn conversations at the interception layer, and ii) a task-tool semantic matching step at the authorization server that evaluates whether the requested tools are appropriate for the extracted tasks. Third, we extend the ASTRA dataset that we introduced in a prior work, by generating novel conversation-tool datasets with multi-turn interactions containing relevant and irrelevant tool calls for a given task. Lastly, we provide the first experimental results for TBAC under multi-turn conversations.
2605.02682.paper_summary_zh: 授權大型語言模型（LLM）驅動的代理動態調用工具並訪問受保護資源會引入重大安全風險，隨著代理參與多輪對話並擴展到分佈式協作，風險會顯著增加。被攻陷或惡意的代理應用程序可能會篡改工具調用、偽造結果或請求超出主體預期任務範圍的權限，這在當前的委派授權流程中可能不會被察覺，因為它們缺乏對原始主體意圖的可見性。鑑於此，我們對持續代理語義授權（CASA）做出以下貢獻。首先，我們提出了一種混合運行時執行模型，結合了由零信任攔截層啟用的確定性和語義控制。五個確定性控制對消息流施加結構和數據完整性保證，而語義檢查層則評估工具調用選擇是否與委託給代理的預期任務一致。其次，與以往基於任務的訪問控制（TBAC）技術僅在單輪交互中運作不同，我們將語義層分解為兩個階段：i）在攔截層提取任務的步驟，從多輪對話中提煉主體的目標，以及 ii）在授權服務器上的任務-工具語義匹配步驟，評估所請求的工具是否適合提取的任務。第三，我們擴展了在先前工作中引入的ASTRA數據集，通過生成包含相關和不相關工具調用的多輪交互的新對話-工具數據集。最後，我們提供了多輪對話下TBAC的首次實驗結果。
2605.02682.author: Majed El Helou et.al.
2605.02682.authors: Majed El Helou, Benjamin Ryder, Chiara Troiani, Jean Diaconu, Hervé Muyal, Marcelo Yannuzzi
